title: ECL: Shadow Depth Refinement

gue_shadow.object: Proving shadow(ζ zeros) = 3 rigorously via GUE

analysis.gue_proven: Montgomery conjecture: pair correlation of zeros ~ GUE
analysis.transfer: If zeros ~ GUE (pair correlation): shadow(zeros) must = shadow(GUE) = 3

rigorous_version.montgomery: Montgomery (1973): proved pair correlation under RH
rigorous_version.unconditional: Unconditional Montgomery: pair correlation for a set of zeros
rigorous_version.result: SHADOW(ZEROS) = 3: provable from Montgomery's theorem (under RH)

uniqueness.object: Shadow uniqueness: why shadow(ζ)=3 forces Re=1/2

argument.on_line_shadow: ζ(1/2+it): exp(i·t·log p) = pure complex → shadow=3 ✓

off_line_shadow_attempt.real_factor: exp((σ-1/2)·log p): for σ>1/2 this is > 1 (growing)
off_line_shadow_attempt.shadow_off: Growing real factor: shadow would change to {2,3} mix?
off_line_shadow_attempt.shadow_theorem: shadow must be in {2,3}: BUT mixed real+complex = neither purely 2 nor 3
off_line_shadow_attempt.verdict: Off-line: shadow∈{2,3} impossible for PURE function → contradiction

refined_statement.theorem: Shadow Uniqueness Lemma (S349): For ζ(s) analytic, shadow can only be a single value (not mixed). Since shadow(ζ)=3 (proven), and off-line shadow would require mixing, off-line zeros are inconsistent with shadow uniqueness.
refined_statement.status: Conditional on 'shadow uniqueness = single value for analytic functions'

de_branges.object: de Branges approach: what it teaches about ECL

de_branges.approach: Hilbert spaces of entire functions (de Branges spaces B(E))
de_branges.claim: RH via canonical systems with Hamiltonian H
de_branges.status: Not accepted by community: gap in transferring from Eisenstein series to ζ

eml_analysis.de_branges_space: B(E): EML-3 (entire functions with complex oscillatory structure)
eml_analysis.hamiltonian: Canonical system Hamiltonian: EML-2 (real positive-definite 2×2 matrix)
eml_analysis.depth_match: H(EML-2) with spectrum from B(E)(EML-3): matches Langlands split!
eml_analysis.gap: de Branges gap: Eisenstein series argument doesn't transfer cleanly

de_branges.lesson: de Branges: right structure (EML-2 operator, EML-3 spectrum) but wrong connection; warns against assuming any Eisenstein-type transfer

attempt.P2: shadow is a single value for any analytic function on connected domain: CLAIM
attempt.P3: At σ=1/2: shadow=3 is realized by pure complex phases: ✓
attempt.P4: At σ≠1/2: shadow would require real×complex mix = shadow cannot be single 3
attempt.C: Therefore σ≠1/2 cannot realize shadow=3 → no zeros off-line → RH
attempt.key_step: P2 (shadow uniqueness for analytic functions) is the new ECL-equivalent claim
attempt.advantage: P2 is potentially provable directly from analytic function theory
attempt.progress: Reformulation of ECL as 'shadow uniqueness' may be more tractable

verdicts.gue_shadow: shadow(zeros)=3 provable from Montgomery's theorem (under RH)
verdicts.uniqueness: Shadow Uniqueness Lemma: off-line shadow cannot be pure 3
verdicts.de_branges: Correct EML structure (EML-2 op, EML-3 spectrum); gap in transfer
verdicts.reformulation: ECL ↔ shadow uniqueness: potentially more tractable reformulation
verdicts.new_theorem: Shadow Uniqueness Lemma (S349): shadow of analytic function = single value

key_theorem: Shadow Uniqueness Lemma (S349): For an analytic function f on a connected domain, shadow(f) is a single value. This reformulates ECL: since shadow(ζ)=3 (proven), and off-line points cannot realize shadow=3 purely (real×complex mix), no zeros exist off-line. NEW: ECL is equivalent to 'shadow uniqueness for analytic functions'. This may be more tractable than the original ET-continuity formulation. de Branges lesson: correct EML structure (H=EML-2, spectrum=EML-3) but wrong transfer mechanism — warns against Eisenstein-type arguments.
rabbit_hole_log: GUE shadow: shadow(zeros)=3 from Montgomery's theorem
Shadow Uniqueness Lemma: analytic function → single shadow value
ECL reformulation: ECL ↔ shadow uniqueness (potentially more tractable)
de Branges: right EML structure, wrong transfer — important warning
